violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener brought to dish machine Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

ROP seafood was observed thawing with the package still in tact. Seafood was observed at 40 degrees. Seafood was disposed of. Guidance document provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap (currently has air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Guidance document provided. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

The dumpster and grease receptacle outside is located on (rocks). The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025

The dumpster outside is located on rocks. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. PIC stated they are in contact with building owner to relocate dumpster to the asphalt/concrete. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Dishwasher to no longer be used until serviced. Inspector demonstrated how to set up 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Various soups were observed held below 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was recently cooked. Food was reheated at the time of inspection. Inspector reviewed proper hot holding procedures with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Veal was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. ROP fish was observed thawing with package in tact. Most were still frozen. One package was above 37 degrees and disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The handsink/soap dispenser by the prep sink was observed to be stored over a knife rack. Knife rack to be moved or soap dispenser to be switched to other side of handsink and splash guard to be added. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Knives were observed stored between prep units. Knives were moved to dishwasher. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The dumpster and grease trap outside is located on rocks. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
